content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 4.5.1-4.5.3 WARN — Push entitlement (aps-environment production) is paired with hourly repeating "unlimited notifications" scheduling and marketing copy explicitly promoting spam-like frequency (App/NotificationScheduler.swift:9-12 "repeats every hour, all day"; fastlane/metadata/en-US/description.txt "alert you EVERY HOUR ... unlimited notifications, all day long").
Pierre: Apple explicitly prohibits using push for unsolicited marketing spam and requires opt-in consent plus an in-app opt-out (4.5.4/4.5.3); an hourly recurring promotional push with no visible opt-in/opt-out UI is a classic rejection pattern. Found: `NotificationScheduler.scheduleHourlyDeals()` schedules a repeating hourly marketing notification, and the store description advertises this as a selling point ("EVERY HOUR", "unlimited"). Fix: throttle frequency, add explicit in-app consent UI and a settings toggle to disable/reduce alerts, and soften the marketing language before submission.
